data.title: Atlanta Fed's Bostic still sees need to keep policy restrictive
data.description:
Atlanta Fed President Raphael Bostic believes that the Federal Reserve should keep its policy rate restrictive, he said on Wednesday.

"We haven't been at target on inflation for many, many years now,"  he said in a moderated conversation at the Atlanta Business Chronicle 2026 Economic Outlook. "We've made great progress, but we still have a distance to go."

Keeping the Fed's interest elevated is the central bank's tool for bringing down inflation. In December, the Federal Open Market Committee lowered its benchmark rate to 3.50%-3.75%, responding to a cooler labor market.

"I think we still need to have a restrictive policy and let things play out," he said. That's due to the expectation that the U.S. economy is expected to pick up, putting upward pressure on prices.

"The inflation challenge is not won yet, and we need to do everything we can" to bring it to target, said Bostic, who is not a voting member on the FOMC this year and who is retiring from the Atlanta Fed in February.

The Fed policymakers must focus on which of its mandates — price stability or maximum employment — is most at risk.  "Labor markets have weakened, but does 'weakening' equal 'weak'?," he asked. In the historical context, a 4.4% unemployment rate is still a solid labor market, he noted.

When asked about his response to the Department of Justice investigating Fed Chair Jerome Powell, Bostic said that Powell's video said "all it needed to say. I have nothing to add."

data.title: Tegna seesaws amid report that FCC Chair may lift local TV cap
data.description:
Tegna (TGNA) quickly rose 3.5%, though is now unchanged amid a report that FCC Chairman Brendan Carr could soon lift the agency's cap on local TV stations. 

Carr could lift the cap within one to three months, according to a Dow Jones report on Wednesday, which cited a research note from Benchmark analyst Daniel Kurnos. Removing the cap would allow for Nexstar Media's (NXST) proposed acquisition of Tegna (TGNA).

"We remain of the opinion that, despite all the noise in the ecosystem, either by cap raise or by waiver, Nexstar will successfully acquire TEGNA, creating massive synergies and an in-market juggernaut," Kurnos wrote. 

Carr has said he's in favor of getting rid of the rules that limit TV stations from owning outlets that reach more than 39% of U.S. households. President Donald Trump in late November appeared to indicate he was opposed to changing the TV ownership cap. 

Nextsar edged higher by 0.6%.

data.title: Mercury General announces dual listing of shares
data.description:
Mercury General (MCY) on Wednesday announced a dual listing of its common stock on NYSE Texas in addition to NYSE, effective January 15, 2026.

The Los Angeles-based multiple-line insurance organization will trade with the same MCY ticker symbol on the NYSE Texas.

"We have a significant business footprint in the Lone Star State, with more than 7% of our total direct written premiums generated in Texas in the past couple of years," said CEO Gabriel Tirador.

"This dual listing reaffirms our support for Texas' business-friendly capital market infrastructure and reinforces our long-standing relationship with the NYSE, while also enhancing shareholder value," added Tirador.

data.title: ClearBridge Large Cap Growth Strategy Q4: Adds Arista Networks, exits Starbucks, shifts restaurant exposure to Chipotle
data.description:
• The ClearBridge Large Cap Growth Strategy lagged the Russell 1000 Growth Index during a major market rally, mainly because it held fewer of the biggest AI stocks and avoided lower-quality AI-related companies.
  • It opened a position in Arista Networks (ANET), citing the company’s relatively small share in the enterprise segment and its strong potential to gain ground as organizations modernize their campus networking infrastructure.
  • It added that it will continue to be active in adding to positions or initiating new ones during pullbacks, such as it did with Palo Alto Networks (PANW) following its announcement of the CyberArk acquisition and Netflix (NFLX) when the stock sold off following disclosure of its intent to purchase Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD).
  • The strategy exited Accenture (ACN) in Q3 and sold Starbucks (SBUX) to focus its restaurant exposure on Chipotle (CMG).
     
  • Source: Press Relase

data.title: Solid Bio rises as Duchenne study gets fully enrolled
data.description:
Solid Biosciences (SLDB) traded higher on Wednesday after the gene therapy developer announced full enrollment in a Phase 1/2 study for its lead candidate SGT-003 in children with a muscle-wasting disorder called Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

The trial named INSPIRE DUCHENNE was previously expected to enroll 50–60 patients across clinical trial sites in the U.S., Canada, Italy, and the U.K.

“We're going to be notifying the sites, hopefully, in the next month or 2 that we're basically not going to take any more recruitment because we filled every possible slot that's possible,” said CEO Alexander Cumbo during the company’s presentation at the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference on Tuesday.

Additionally, Solid Bio (SLDB) provided an interim update from the study noting that as of Jan. 9, SGT-003 has been generally well tolerated among 33 patients who received dosing, adding to a previous safety update that represented 23 patients as of Oct. 31, 2025.

Additional data from the study, as well as an update on the company’s interactions with the FDA regarding a Phase 3 confirmatory trial design for SGT-003, are expected in mid-2026.

data.title: Gevo awarded patent for ethanol-to-olefins process to produce renewable jet fuel
data.description:
Gevo (GEVO) +2.2% in Wednesday's trading after saying it was awarded a patent for its ethanol-to-olefins technology to produce renewable jet fuel and chemicals, potentially lowering operating costs by as much as 35% compared to current technologies.

Gevo (GEVO) said the patented process produces light olefins from ethanol and can convert those olefins into transportation fuels using commercially proven alcohol-to-jet technologies; olefins can be used to make cost-effective renewable chemical building blocks such as bio-propylene.

Gevo (GEVO) is developing ethanol-to-olefins technology with partners LG Chem to deploy a next-generation ETO process for renewable chemical applications and with Axens for fuel applications.

"We continue to build an intellectual property portfolio that we believe will put Gevo in a cost-leadership position on [alcohol-to-jet technologies] for years to come," Gevo (GEVO) President Paul Bloom said.

data.title: Virtu, Optiver partner to challenge Citadel Securities in US options flow - report
data.description:
Virtu Financial (VIRT), Akuna Capital, Belvedere Trading, and Optiver are investing in a firm that will buy U.S. options order flow from retail brokers, a market dominated by Ken Griffin's Citadel Securities, according to a media report on Wednesday.

The four companies have invested in a new options intermediary, Optimal Market Technologies, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter. The four market makers will lead a larger group of liquidity providers, who will compete for flow routed from trading apps used by small-scale traders to Optimal, the article said.

Optimal has received approval from the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority for this system. But it hasn't yet received final approval from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to create an alternative trading system, an electronic platform that matches buyers with sellers.

U.S. retail brokers, such as Robinhood Markets (HOOD) and Morgan Stanley's (MS) E*Trade — which cut their trading commissions to as low as zero — make most of their money by selling their customers orders to wholesalers. Order flow payments across equities and options climbed 51% to $1.24B in Q3 2025 from a year earlier, according to analysis from Bloomberg Intelligence.

data.title: Impinj sells off even after strong guidance update
data.description:
• Impinj (PI) shares fell more than 6% in late morning trading on Wednesday, even as the company provided a guidance update for its fourth-quarter that was stronger-than-expected. 
  • The Seattle-based company now expects fourth-quarter revenue to come in near the high end of its prior $90Mto $93M guidance range, compared to the analyst estimate of $91.9M. Impinj also said it expects adjusted EBITDA to exceed the midpoint of its previously issued $15.4M–16.9M guidance.
  • Chris Diorio, Impinj co-founder and CEO, and Cary Baker, Impinj CFO, are appearing at a Needham investment conference on Wednesday.
  • The company is set to report its full-year results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, on February 5.

data.title: Winners and losers from the November Retail Sales Report
data.description:
Retail sales were up 0.6% month-over-month in November and were 3.3% higher than a year ago, according to the U.S. Census Bureau. The retail sales growth was higher than the 0.4% increase economists projected. Core retail sales, which feed into the calculation for consumer spending for GDP for the quarter, rose 0.4% after improving by a downwardly revised 0.6% in October.

Sales were up across most categories for the beginning of the holiday season. Clothing stores stood out with a 7.5% year-over-year jump in a positive sign for mall retailers such as Gap (GAP), Urban Outfitters (URBN), American Eagle Outfitters (AEO), and Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF). Once again, the health & personal care stores category was strong with 6.7% growth, which may bode well for chains such as Walgreens, Rite Aid, Ulta Beauty (ULTA), Sally Beauty (SBH), and National Vision Holdings (EYE). The nonstore retailers category outperformed with a 7.2% increase in November from a year ago. The holiday month is important for e-commerce players included in the category, such as Amazon (AMZN), Wayfair (W), and Etsy (ETSY).

The auto dealer group was an underperformer in November, with a 1.1% decline from a year ago. Some names included in that tally are Carvana (CVNA), CarMax (KMX), Lithia Motors (LAD), AutoNation (AN), Asbury Automotive Group (ABG), CarGurus (CARG), Group 1 Automotive (GPI), Sonic Automotive (SAH), and Penske Automotive Group (PAG)

"Altogether, it's a fairly solid report pointing to a resilient consumer despite signs of eroding consumer confidence, which has dropped to recession levels, worries about inflation, and a slowing labor market," summarized KPMG Senior Economist Ken Kim on the report.

data.title: U.S. stocks see continued declines as traders digest PPI, retail sales
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages saw another day of declines on Wednesday after the wholesale inflation data came in line with consensus, while retail sales were hotter than expected.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.8% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.3%.

The U.S. Producer Price Index rose in line with consensus in November, while retail sales jumped past consensus for that month.

Moreover, both business inventories and retail inventories in October rose higher than expected, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

“Wednesday's PPI, while delayed, helps to confirm that the inflation data remained stable even during the data drought of the government shutdown and helps to justify the Federal Reserve's rate cut in December, which was initiated largely because of softening jobs data rather than worries about inflation,” said Clark Bellin, president and CIO at Bellwether Wealth.

“Stocks are off to an encouraging start so far in 2026, even with the barrage of political and geopolitical headlines out of Washington. Volatility remains subdued even with a market that is facing seemingly endless headlines, which shows that investors have largely become desensitized to the day-to-day movements out of Washington,” Bellin added.

In addition, the current account deficit narrowed more than expected in Q3 2025. 

Lastly, U.S. existing home sales rose 5.1% MoM to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 4.35M in December vs. 4.230M consensus and 4.14M prior, the National Association of Realtors said.

Over in the bond market, Treasury yields moved lower across the curve, with the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) 4 basis points lower to 4.14%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) 4 basis points lower to 3.51%.

The U.S. Supreme Court was once again expected to release a ruling on the legality of President Donald Trump’s tariffs, but there was no ruling issued.

On earnings, Wells Fargo (WFC) -4.6% reported weaker than expected revenue for the fourth quarter, while Bank of America (BAC) topped Wall Street expectations, still -4.4%.

“We believe the upcoming tech earnings reports at the end of January and early February will be the most important in recent memory. Tech was the best performing sector of 2025 and the guidance that tech companies release in the coming weeks will be important to determine whether or not tech can have another banner year in 2026,” Bellin said.

More on markets
